explicit_text: Lorser Feitelson and I shared a similar approach when it came to our biographies. We both believed that private lives should remain private and avoided pandering to people's curiosity. Our biographies are brief, focusing mainly on professional details like exhibitions, which presents us in a professional and somewhat anonymous way. This stance was something we established together and have stuck to over time.
implicit_text: I shared a similar approach with a fellow artist when it came to our biographies. We both believed that private lives should remain private and avoided pandering to people's curiosity. Our biographies are brief, focusing mainly on professional details like exhibitions, which presents us in a professional and somewhat anonymous way. This stance was something we established together and have stuck to over time.

explicit_text: I remember very little about Chicago since I was only four and a half when my family moved away in December 1912. Most of my memories are of indoor spaces because, at that time, there were many kidnappings and children had to be kept under close watch. We lived in a flat building with a porch where I recall a woman handing me a present using an umbrella. Chicago's fierce climate and black fogs also contributed to my mother's poor health, which was a major reason for our move.
implicit_text: I remember very little about the city where I lived until I was four and a half, since my family moved away in December 1912. Most of my memories are of indoor spaces because, at that time, there were many kidnappings and children had to be kept under close watch. We lived in a flat building with a porch where I recall a woman handing me a present using an umbrella. The area's fierce climate and black fogs also contributed to my mother's poor health, which was a major reason for our move.

explicit_text: I grew up in Los Angeles after moving from Chicago as a child. Living here felt very different because Los Angeles is part of the West and the Pacific Coast, with open spaces like mountains and meadows around Pasadena where I lived. This environment shaped my memories and my experience as an artist working in this region. The contrast between the closed-in feeling of Chicago and the openness of Los Angeles was striking to me.
implicit_text: I grew up in a major West Coast city after moving from the Midwest as a child. Living here felt very different because this city is part of the West and the Pacific Coast, with open spaces like mountains and meadows around the nearby suburb where I lived. This environment shaped my memories and my experience as an artist working in this region. The contrast between the closed-in feeling of my previous city and the openness of this place was striking to me.

explicit_text: Living on the Pacific Coast, particularly in the Los Angeles area, gave me a sense of openness and natural beauty that was very different from my early years in Chicago. The Pacific Coast represents the West to me, with its mountains and meadows that I grew up with in Pasadena. This setting influenced my perspective as an artist and my connection to the region. It was a significant change from the closed-in, indoor memories I had from Chicago.
implicit_text: Living along the western coastline, particularly in the area around a major Southern California city, gave me a sense of openness and natural beauty that was very different from my early years in the Midwest. This coastal region represents the West to me, with its mountains and meadows that I grew up with in a nearby suburb. This setting influenced my perspective as an artist and my connection to the region. It was a significant change from the closed-in, indoor memories I had from my earlier home.

explicit_text: My family moved to Pasadena, California, in December 1912 mainly for my mother's health because Chicago's climate was harsh and caused her bronchitis. In Pasadena, I grew up surrounded by mountains and a big meadow across the street, which was a stark contrast to my earlier life in Chicago. My father's cousin already lived there, which probably helped with the move. Pasadena became the place where I developed my memories and identity as an artist in the West.
implicit_text: My family moved to a Southern California suburb in December 1912 mainly for my mother's health because the harsh climate of our previous city caused her bronchitis. There, I grew up surrounded by mountains and a big meadow across the street, which was a stark contrast to my earlier life in the Midwest. My father's cousin already lived there, which probably helped with the move. This suburb became the place where I developed my memories and identity as an artist in the West.
